steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage opens by noting Joplin is celebrated for 'Maple Leaf Rag' and 'The Entertainer,' then pivots with 'However' to argue listeners miss his lesser-known works by overlooking them. The main purpose is what the whole passage is trying to accomplish, not just one detail.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — To argue that more attention should be given to Joplin's lesser known works.: matches the passage's core argument, signaled by 'However' and the concluding 'These masterpieces deserve as much fame' — ✓
  • Choice B — To describe the similarities and differences between ragtime music and opera.: a secondary detail used as evidence, not the main purpose — ✗
  • Choice C — To encourage music lovers to listen to music by many different composers.: too broad — the passage focuses only on Joplin — ✗
  • Choice D — To explain how Joplin learned to compose and perform ragtime music.: not discussed anywhere in the passage — ✗
A is correct.
